primary_accounting_issue: La prise en compte du risque de non-exécution dans la mesure de la juste valeur, notamment le risque de crédit de la contrepartie pour un actif et le risque de non-performance propre pour un passif.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule by selecting the single IFRS standard that directly governs fair value measurement and related risk assumptions; no other provided document directly governs this same measurement issue at an overlapping level.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty because the question is explicitly about fair value measurement and IFRS 13 is the dedicated governing standard for that topic.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether and how fair value reflects market-participant assumptions about credit risk and non-performance risk.

peripheral_authority.reason: This document addresses share-based payment measurement, not the general fair value non-performance-risk issue raised here.
This document addresses recoverable amount and value in use, which is related to valuation but not to identifying peer fair value approaches for counterparty non-performance risk.
This document addresses estimation uncertainty and accounting estimates generally, not the governing fair value treatment itself.

treatment_families.family: Fair value of an asset incorporating market-participant assumptions about counterparty credit and other risk
Fair value of a liability or own equity instrument incorporating non-performance risk including own credit risk

approaches.label: Fair value of an asset
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 13 requires fair value to reflect market-participant assumptions about risk, which can include the counterparty's likelihood of non-performance when pricing an asset.

approaches.label: Fair value of a liability
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 13 explicitly requires the fair value of a liability to reflect non-performance risk, including the issuer's own credit risk.
